name: 7 Summits Elevation Challenge
description: A running race in Any City - Any State, TX.

name: Race Through Texas 1M, 5K, 10K, 13.1, 26.2 - Now Only $20!
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Brazos Bend 50
description: Trail Racing Over Texas's flagship April event at Brazos Bend State Park, offering distances from 5K to 50 miles on one of the flattest trail courses in Texas. The 50K serves as the 2026 RRCA Texas State Championships. All races start and finish at Elm Lake Pavilion among the park's famous alligators and wildlife.

name: Bellaire Trolley Run
description: The 30th Annual Bellaire Trolley Run starts in front of Bellaire City Hall with a 1-mile division at 7:30 AM and the 5K at 8:00 AM, plus a wheelchair 5K and stroller 5K. A beloved community tradition, all finishers receive a medal and t-shirt, followed by a post-race party.

name: Spring Fling - Houston
description: A multi-distance spring running event in the south Houston area by Bigfoot Running Company, offering a Kids Dash, 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and half marathon. The course location is TBD in the Houston 77047 area.

name: Race for the Dome
description: The 6th Annual Race for the Dome celebrates the Astrodome's 61st birthday with a 5K road course through NRG Park and a Kids 1K fun run. Participants are encouraged to wear vintage gear or Dome attire; the event benefits the Astrodome Conservancy.

name: Run for the Rose 5K
description: A 5K road race in Missouri City in Fort Bend County, part of a springtime rose-themed community running event.

name: AGPMA Run for the Goal 5K
description: The 13th Annual AGPMA Run for the Goal 5K takes place at Challenger Seven Memorial Park in Webster, benefiting Al Garza's Premier Martial Arts Black Belt Candidate class. A community-focused fundraiser 5K open to runners and walkers of all abilities.

name: Project Joy and Hope Fun Run
description: The 4th Annual Project Joy and Hope 5K Fun Run/Walk takes place at the San Jacinto Monument with stunning views of the San Jacinto Battleground. Proceeds support Project Joy and Hope, a nonprofit dedicated to helping seriously ill children and their families in the greater Houston area.

name: Sienna Run for the Rosé 5K
description: A chip-timed 5K through the Sienna master-planned community centered around Sawmill Lake in Missouri City, with a finish-line rosé and brunch celebration. Entry benefits Child Advocates of Fort Bend, a local nonprofit serving foster children.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
description: A free, weekly timed 5K community run held every Saturday at 8:00 AM in Terry Hershey Park along the Buffalo Bayou trail in west Houston. Open to runners of all abilities with no pre-registration required; results are published online for all finishers.

name: Houston Empower Walk & 5K Run
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: Sheldon Shuffle 5K
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Run for the Rose 2026
description: Annual charity 5K and family 1K benefiting brain cancer research through the Dr. Marnie Rose Foundation. The certified course winds through Helix Park in the Texas Medical Center.

name: Saint Arnold Art Car IPA 5K
description: Beer-themed 5K at Saint Arnold Brewing Company celebrating Houston's Art Car Parade. Entry includes a finisher medal, pint glass, and three Saint Arnold beverages.

name: Vintage Park 13.1
description: A half marathon road race in the Vintage Park area of northwest Houston, running through the Champions/Willowbrook corridor.

name: B52 Brewing 5K Beer Run
description: 5K beer run on a flat out-and-back course starting and finishing at B52 Brewing in Conroe. Part of the Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge series with post-race tastings included.

name: Bearkat Bash
description: 20th annual 5K run and 1K kids fun run at Klein High School benefiting the Klein High School Cross Country team and Klein ISD programs.

name: Gulf Coast Grind
description: Beach ultramarathon event with distances from 5K to 100K on hard-packed sand along Surfside Beach. Organized by Rise Racing Co. with a 20-hour cutoff for the longest distance.

name: Steps for Vets 5K - Run | Walk | Roll
description: Second annual peer-to-peer fundraising 5K at Sam Houston Race Park supporting U.S.VETS' mission to prevent and end veteran homelessness in the Houston area.

name: Sisters Network 16th Annual Stop the Silence 5K Walk/Run
description: Sisters Network's 16th annual 5K walk/run at Tom Bass Regional Park raising awareness about breast cancer among African American women. Includes a kids walk/run option.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
description: A free, weekly timed 5K community run held every Saturday at 8:00 AM in Terry Hershey Park along the Buffalo Bayou trail in west Houston. Open to runners of all abilities with no pre-registration required; results are published online for all finishers.

name: XKLusive '06K Race/Walk
description: The Alexandria Foundation's '06K Race adds an extra 1K to a standard 5K to honor the 1906 founding year of Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc., held at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land. Now in its fifth year, 2026 proceeds benefit local Autism nonprofits Hope for Three and Seensory and Me Ministries.

name: SJH Sabercat Stampede
description: A running race in Katy, TX.

name: Space City Gym 5K/10K
description: Third annual 5K and 10K through the trails and pathways of Jesse Jones Park in Humble, organized by Space City Gym. Course winds through lush trails and open pathways.

name: Pirate 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park
description: Multi-distance loop course race at Dow Park in Deer Park organized by US Road Running. Participants are encouraged to run in pirate-themed costumes for prize consideration.

name: Space City Half Marathon, 10K, 5K & Mile
description: Inaugural spring half marathon on a fast, flat course through Houston's westside inner-loop, starting and finishing at Delmar-Tuso Sports Complex. Also the finale of the Lone Star Race Series.

name: Running Through the Decades Half Marathon - Houston
description: 80s-themed multi-distance road race in Sugar Land featuring a costume contest, live DJ, and post-race celebration. Organized by Bigfoot Running Company with distances from 1 mile to half marathon.

name: Inaugural Kalayaan 5K Fun Run
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Bayou Bash Relay
description: A 4-person x 2.2-mile relay on paved and crushed granite trails at T.C. Jester Park, followed by the HARRA Family Picnic. Part of the HARRA Spring Race Series, hosted by Bayou City Road Runners.

name: DoNot Stop Half Marathon (5k/10k) - Houston
description: A donut-themed half marathon, 10K, 5K, 1-mile, and Kids Dash held at John Paul Landing Park & Education Center in Cypress. Every participant receives a designer shirt, finisher medal, chip timing with live results, and free race-day photos, plus a donut waiting at the finish line.

name: Galveston Island Brewing Co. 5K Beer Run
description: Chip-timed 5K starting and finishing at Galveston Island Brewing, running along the Galveston Seawall. Part of the Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge series. Entry includes a finisher glass, four 8-oz beer tastings, and a tech tee. Benefits the Galveston Island Humane Society.

name: Smilin' Rylen Fun Run
description: Annual charity run at the University of Houston at Katy campus supporting the Smilin' Rylen Foundation, which raises awareness about organ donation and assists families in crisis. Offers 5K, 10K, and 1-mile distances; 10K and 5K start at 8:15 AM, 1-mile at approximately 9:00 AM.

name: Strides for Schools 5K Fun Run/Walk
description: Annual community 5K fun run/walk at Clyde Abshier Stadium presented by Shell Deer Park, with 100% of registration proceeds benefiting the Deer Park Education Foundation.

name: Coffee Lovers Run 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: Coffee-themed group run at Eleanor Tinsley Park along Allen Parkway. Participants receive a finisher medal, running t-shirt, and giveaway. Open to all paces and ages.

name: Divas Galveston Half Marathon and 5K
description: All-women's half marathon and 5K along Galveston's scenic Seawall with ocean views. Every participant receives a designer race shirt, finisher medal, tutu, and tiara. A portion of proceeds benefits a local women's charity.

name: Vintage Half Marathon, 5K & Kid's K
description: Part of the Run Houston Race Series presented by Houston Methodist. Starting at Klein High School, this event features a half marathon, timed and untimed 5K, and a Kid's 1K.

name: Biz Run
description: A running race in Sugarland, TX.

name: Move for MDS
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Run Like a Diva
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Girls on the Run Greater Houston Celebratory 5K
description: Spring season celebratory 5K at Rice University. Participants walk, roll, push, run or skip at their happy pace to celebrate the Girls on the Run program season.

name: Klein ISD Education Foundation 5K & Fun Run
description: USATF certified community 5K at Klein Memorial Stadium. Proceeds support Klein Education Foundation teacher classroom grants for innovative academic projects.

name: The Pear Run
description: 20th annual community race at Independence Park benefiting the Pearland Citizens Police Academy Alumni Association and the Pearland Police Department. Out-and-back course along Pearland Parkway.

name: Cinco De Mayo Run 5K/10K/13.1 HOUSTON
description: A Cinco de Mayo-themed race offering half marathon, 10K, 5K, and family 1-mile fun run distances along the Bayou Loop Trails in west Houston. Virtual run options are also available for those who prefer to participate remotely.

name: 2026 Preventive Medicine Initiative: Heart & Sole 5K
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Astros Race to the Pennant presented by Houston Methodist
description: Annual race starting and finishing at Daikin Park (home of the Houston Astros). Features a 10K, 5K, adapted divisions, and Orbit's Kids Dash, with a post-race celebration on the ballpark concourse.

name: Fuzzy's Taco Dash
description: A running race in Kingwood, TX.

name: RUN FOR HOPE
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: Run Like A Warrior 5k -Houston Area
description: A running race in Tomball, TX.

name: Z Krewe's 0.5K
description: Tongue-in-cheek 0.5K charity fun run at Galveston Bay Brewing requiring zero training. All race trappings packed into 1,640 feet, raising money for the Galveston County Food Bank Kidz Pacz Program.

name: El Cinco Cinco
description: Cinco de Mayo-themed race hosted at Karbach Brewing Co featuring a 5K, 10K, 1-mile, and Kids & Family 1K. Post-race fiesta includes music, a Cinco de Mayo market, and beverages.

name: Fuzzy's Taco Dash Sugar Land
description: Cinco de Mayo-themed 5K, 10K, relay, and kids run in Sugar Land in partnership with Fuzzy's Tacos & Margs. Post-race festivities include food and celebration.

name: The Cinco Cinco 5K, 10K & Kids 1K
description: Annual Cinco de Mayo celebration hosted by Houston Running Co. at Karbach Brewing Company. Participants are encouraged to dress in fiesta attire and enjoy a post-race Cinco de Mayo market. Proceeds benefit Ainsley's Angels Southeast Texas, The Asher House Animal Sanctuary, and LifeGift Organ Donation.

name: Texas Crab Festival 5K
description: Timed 5K on a certified course at Swedes Park on the Bolivar Peninsula, kicking off the annual Texas Crab Festival. Includes a Kids 1K. Participants are encouraged to wear crab costumes.

name: YCF 5K Fun Run
description: A 5K fun run on Galveston Island organized by the Young Champions Foundation.

name: 29th Annual Sprint for Life 5K Run/Walk
description: Charity 5K at Helix Park in the Texas Medical Center benefiting ovarian cancer research at MD Anderson Cancer Center. Since 1998, Sprint for Life has raised over $5.8 million for the Blanton-Davis Ovarian Cancer Research Program. Includes a Sprint for Sprouts/Tot Trot kids race at 9:15 AM.

name: Yaga's Children's Fund WILD 5K Fun Run
description: A 5K fun run at Moody Gardens in Galveston kicking off Yaga's Wild Game & BBQ Cook-Off weekend. All proceeds benefit Yaga's Children's Fund, supporting charities that serve the children of Galveston County. Post-race BBQ, live music, and vendors follow at 11 AM.

name: The Race to Grand Oaks
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: Hometown Half Marathon & 5K/10K - Houston
description: Family-friendly race at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land featuring chip-timed events from a Kids Dash through a half marathon. Includes pacers for the half marathon, free photos, designer shirts, and custom finisher medals.

name: Legacy of Courage Half Marathon - Houston
description: Race at Burroughs Park in Tomball honoring disabled veterans. All proceeds benefit The Outdoor Disabled Veterans Program (The ODV) through Knowledge Point Network. Also includes a quarter marathon distance.

name: 5K Mamas Fun Run
description: A running race in Missouri City, TX.

name: Houston Humane Society 44th Annual K9 Fun Run + Walk
description: Annual charity fun run and walk benefiting the Houston Humane Society, featuring a 3.2K dog-friendly course through the Sawyer Yards area. Includes a timed competitive run, alumni parade of adopted pets, and costume contest.

name: Santa Fe Ten Memorial Run In Remembrance
description: Annual memorial run at Jack Brooks Park honoring the ten victims of the May 18, 2018 Santa Fe High School shooting. Proceeds support the Santa Fe Ten Memorial Foundation, building a permanent memorial.

name: Terlingua Track Club LP Run
description: Billed as the oldest race in Texas, the Terlingua Track Club LP Run at Rice University has all participants run as far as possible in exactly 33 minutes and 20 seconds. A HARRA Spring Series event.

name: Operation: Ruck the Bayou
description: Second annual 5K and 10K run/ruck at Saint Arnold Brewery benefiting The Joshua Chamberlain Society's Houston Chapter, supporting severely wounded veterans and families of fallen service members.

name: The Wiener Run 5K & 10K - Houston
description: A spring running event at Oyster Creek Park featuring 10K, 5K, 1 Mile, and Kids Dash distances. Supports a local wiener dog rescue group, with finishers receiving complimentary hot dogs, medals, and live music.

name: Mindy Moses 5K Memorial Fun Run
description: A running race in Bellville, TX.

name: Run Houston! Clear Lake 5K & 10K presented by Houston Methodist
description: Part of the Run Houston! Race Series presented by Houston Methodist. The third race in the Spring Series features 10K, 5K, and Kids 1K distances on an out-and-back course starting at the University of Houston-Clear Lake campus.

name: Fit Foodie Family 5K & Kids' 1K
description: Family-friendly race at Sugar Land Town Square benefiting Kids' Meals, Inc. Features a post-race festival with live entertainment, recovery services, food vendors, and family activities.

name: Daily News Press Run - Texas City
description: A running race in Texas City, TX.

name: Run To Rebuild Texas - 5k Fun Run
description: A running race in New Caney, TX.

name: Memorial Day Run 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: Small-wave community group run at Bayou Loop Trails honoring fallen service members. Optional timing, all paces and ages welcome.

name: Memorial Day 10K/5K
description: Houston Running Co's Holiday Series race honoring U.S. military members who gave their lives in service. The scenic course runs down Memorial Drive from St. Thomas High School, followed by a patriotic post-race celebration with music and food.

name: Memorial Day Hero Salute
description: 2nd annual event in Kingwood's Town Center Park honoring military heroes, benefiting Semper Fi & America's Fund and Impact a Hero. Also features unique Murph and Loredo fitness challenges and a kids tug-of-war.

name: Pasadena Police Department Superhero 5K / Kids 1K
description: Annual community superhero-themed 5K and Kids 1K hosted by the Pasadena Police Department at the Police Academy. Participants are encouraged to dress as their favorite superhero. Registration includes a swag bag and medal.

name: Tejas Beer 5K
description: Part of the Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge series. The out-and-back course winds through the brewery neighborhood, finishing at Tejas Brewery with a custom finisher glass, four beer tastings, and post-race festivities in the taproom.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
address: Terry Hershey Park, west of Eldridge Parkway
description: Free weekly timed 5K held every Saturday morning on the paved Terry Hershey Park trail. Open to all abilities — runners, joggers, and walkers welcome. Registration required but no cost.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K, & Half Marathon at Dow Park
description: US Road Running's recurring Medal Madness series at Dow Park in Deer Park. The out-and-back course through the park offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon distances. Finishers choose from a selection of themed medals.

name: The Big Run with Fleet Feet Greenbriar
description: Community 5K celebrating Global Running Day, presented by Brooks at Fleet Feet Greenbriar. A festive evening run with summer vibes and shared memories.

name: Lemonade Dash Houston
description: Part of the Summer Dash Series by Houston Running Co., the Lemonade Dash is a family-friendly 5K/10K and Kids K held at Karbach Brewing Co. Participants receive a custom bib, finisher medal, and a signature cup of lemonade.

name: Miles for Miracles: 5K for NICU Families
description: A 5K fun run supporting the Harmonie Grace Foundation and families of micro preemies. Family-friendly event open to all ages and fitness levels.

name: Run for Breast Cancer 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: A charity race raising awareness for breast cancer, held on paved bayou trails in west Houston. Offers wave starts across multiple distances in a supportive community atmosphere.

name: Big Rivers Splash & Dash
description: 2nd annual race through Big Rivers Waterpark and around the lake, benefiting the TANGO2 Research Foundation. Features 5K, 10K, and Kids Dash (ages 10 and under).

name: Outrun Hunger
description: Trail run at Grand Central Park presented by H-E-B, benefiting Montgomery County Food Bank. Includes timed 5K and 8K trail runs plus a 1K Color Dash for kids.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
description: A free, weekly, timed 5K run held every Saturday at Terry Hershey Park. The paved out-and-back course runs west of Eldridge Parkway. Open to all, organized entirely by volunteers.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (24)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy. Offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Barn Burner
description: A summer race at Sugar Land Memorial Park offering distances from a Kids' Dash through a half marathon. Features custom finisher medals and age group awards across 17 divisions.

name: Houston Stars & Stripes Half Marathon, 10K & 5K
description: A patriotic-themed half marathon, 10K, and 5K at Atascocita High School in Humble benefiting the PTSD Foundation of America, with runners receiving an American flag to carry across the finish line. Race 6 of 9 in the Run Houston Race Series.

name: No Label Brewing 1st Street 5K Beer Run
description: Chip-timed beer run starting and finishing at No Label Brewing in downtown Katy. Part of the 2026 Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge. Entry includes a finisher glass, four 8-oz tastings, and a race tee.

name: Run Juneteenth Day 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: Juneteenth celebration run on Bayou Loop Trails with small-group waves and optional timing. Celebrates freedom, community, and the unbreakable human spirit.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & Half Marathon at Dow Park
description: A US Road Running Medal Madness event at Dow Park in Deer Park. Offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon distances with finisher medals for all participants.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
description: A free, weekly, timed 5K run held every Saturday at Terry Hershey Park. The paved out-and-back course runs west of Eldridge Parkway. Open to all, organized entirely by volunteers.

name: Independence Day 8K & 5K
description: 10th annual early Independence Day celebration featuring 8K, 5K, and Kids/Family 1K at 9:00 AM. Part of the Houston Holiday Series at Meyerland Plaza.

name: The Pride Run Houston
description: Houston's first intentionally LGBTQIA-inclusive 5K and 10K run, uniting the community through fitness and celebration. Includes a 1-mile walk option and post-race party.

name: Red, White and Blue Half Marathon Houston
description: Patriotic-themed road race through Sugar Land hosted by Bigfoot Running Company. Also offers 2-mile, 4-mile, and 8-mile distances. Fast and flat course.

name: Firecracker 5K
description: A one-time-only, USATF-sanctioned evening 5K celebrating America's 250th anniversary, held along The Woodlands Waterway on July 2. Produced by The Woodlands Township Parks and Recreation Department, the chip-timed course starts at 7 p.m. with no race-day registration available.

name: Houston Fourth Fest Road Race
description: The 2nd annual Houston Fourth Fest features a flat, mostly shaded July 4th road race at Vintage Park in NW Houston with distances of 5K, 9K, 17.76K (commemorating 1776), and a family 1.776K fun run. The event also hosts Houston's Beer Mile Championship and Hot Dog Eating Contest Championship post-race.

name: Run Wild 5K & Kids Run
description: Annual July 4th 5K and 0.6-mile Kids Run in the Galleria/Uptown area. Benefits Special Olympics Texas and Girls on the Run Greater Houston. Kids Run starts at 7:10 AM. Includes finisher medal and post-race refreshments from New Magnolia Brewing Co.

name: Texas Bud Heat Wave 5K & 5 Miler
description: Annual July 4th road race in Mont Belvieu offering 5K and 5-mile distances. Out-and-back course on flat concrete and asphalt with multiple water/cooling stations. Starts at Wismer Distributing. Post-race Mont Belvieu 4th of July Celebration with food, rides, and music.

name: July 4th Run 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: An Independence Day celebration run at Eleanor Tinsley Park offering multiple distance options for all fitness levels. Features a smaller private group format with wave starts, finisher medals, and t-shirts.

name: Kingwood Texas 4th of July Freedom Run
description: 3rd annual Independence Day run through the Kingwood Greenbelt Trails at Town Center Park. Offers 8-mile, 4-mile, and 2-mile distances plus a Kids Dash (100-200m sprint, ages 10 and under). Kids Dash at 6:45 AM. Supports Team RWB and Torch of Freedom Foundation.

name: BASF Firecracker 4
description: Long-running Independence Day tradition in Lake Jackson hosted by LJFAT (Lake Jackson Freeport Area Triathletes). Features a 4-mile run, 2-mile walk, and Kids Run (Kids Run at 7:15 AM). Starts at Dunbar Park.

name: Run for Chocolate 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: A chocolate-themed community run at Eleanor Tinsley Park welcoming all paces and ages, with optional timing and swag packages including shirt, medal, and finisher towel. Features multiple wave starts and a family-friendly 1-mile fun run.

name: July 4th Freedom Run 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
address: Brays Bayou Greenway at Hermann Park
description: A patriotic community run along Brays Bayou Greenway near Hermann Park with 5K, 10K, half marathon, and 1-mile family fun run options. 10% of net proceeds plus 100% of fundraising goes to Hope for the Warriors.

name: Treat Trot Houston
description: A family-friendly summer road race organized by Bodies Race Company featuring chip-timed 5K and 10K distances. Participants receive a designer shirt, custom medal, and snacks at the finish line.

name: Coastal Plains Relay
description: A 75-mile team relay race from Katy to Surfside Beach across scenic backroads. Teams of up to 10 runners tackle designated legs, with a rucking division starting the evening before.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (28)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this summer race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon distances on a flat out-and-back course with a 3.5-hour time limit. Chip-timed with finisher medals; no on-site registration available.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (28)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this weekly race at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress features 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with a 3.5-hour time limit. No on-site registration; chip-timed with finisher medals.

name: Popsicle Dash Houston
description: The 7th annual Popsicle Dash is the second race in Houston Running Co's three-part summer series alongside the Lemonade Dash and Watermelon Dash. The course starts and finishes at Karbach Brewing Co with post-race festivities including food, drinks, and music.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (29)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this weekly race at Dow Park in Deer Park offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with a 3.5-hour time limit. Chip-timed with finisher medals; no on-site registration.

name: Lunar Landing 5K & Kids 1K
description: A community 5K and kids 1K at the Clear Lake Recreation and Community Center celebrating the Apollo 11 moon landing anniversary. Supports charitable giving initiatives.

name: Red, White and Blue Half Marathon - Houston
description: Hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Kingdom Builders Center in southwest Houston, this summer race offers a half marathon, 8-mile, 4-mile, 2-mile, and kids' dash with chip timing and custom medals. Top finishers in the longer distances earn a coveted Bigfoot trophy.

name: Eagle 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (30)
description: Part of the US Road Running Eagle Series, this weekly race at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Triple Dog Dare
description: A summer road race at Sugar Land Memorial Park offering 5K, 10K, and 15K distances with chip timing, custom finisher medals, and age group awards four deep. Runners who register by July 18 are guaranteed their shirt size.

name: Christmas in July 5K & 10K Houston
description: A Christmas-themed summer 5K and 10K in Houston. Part of the seasonal themed race series popular in the Houston area.

name: Shark Week Showdown 5K, 10K - Galveston, TX
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Run for Coffee Lovers 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: A coffee-themed community run at Eleanor Tinsley Park for all paces and ages, with optional timing and swag packages including shirt, medal, and finisher towel. Features a family-friendly 1-mile fun run option.

name: Terrain Race | Houston
description: A 5K obstacle race through the natural terrain of Three Palms Action Sports Park north of Houston, featuring 20+ obstacles including mud, ropes, monkey bars, and walls. Runners select a start wave and compete on the same course used for all ability levels.

name: Heatwave Half Marathon Houston
description: Summer half marathon with 10K, 5K, 1 mile, and kids dash options. Features chip timing, custom finisher medals, designer shirts, free photos, and complimentary ice cream at the finish line.

name: Watermelon Dash Houston
description: Seventh annual Watermelon Dash, the final race in Houston Running Co.'s Summer Dash Series. Held at Karbach Brewing Co. with 5K, 10K, and kids/family fun run distances.

name: Houston Party in the Park
description: Free HARRA community running event at Memorial Park featuring a 5K with Houston Striders, 10K loop with Houston Harriers, and kids runs with 1-mile and 3-mile options. Includes live music, free refreshments, and raffles.

name: Ninja 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (32)
description: Part of the US Road Running series, this weekly race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Southern Star Brewing Co. 5K Beer Run
description: Event #6 in the Run In Texas 5K Six Pack Beer Challenge, this annual 5K beer run starts and finishes at Southern Star Brewing Company in Conroe. Benefiting Toys for Tots, the flat course winds through the Sunset Ridge neighborhood; entry includes a souvenir pint glass and post-race beer tastings.

name: Summer Meltdown Half Marathon Houston
description: Annual summer half marathon at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land featuring a scenic course through parks and natural landscapes. Organized by Bigfoot Running Company, the fast and flat course includes pavement and crushed granite paths with a sunrise start.

name: Run for PB&J 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: Themed road race through Houston organized by The Best Races, a nationwide race series operating since 2014. Exact start/finish location is emailed to registered participants the week of the race.

name: Run for Mental Health 5K/10K/13.1 Houston
description: Charity road race supporting mental health awareness, organized by The Best Races. Participants run through Houston streets with 5K, 10K, and half marathon options. Exact start/finish location is provided to registrants the week of the event.

name: Habanero Hundred
description: Trail Racing Over Texas ultra event at 7IL Ranch near Cat Spring. Races start at high noon in August heat on a sandy course with average temperatures above 100°F. Distances range from 10K to 135 miles with a 30-hour cutoff. Buckles for 100-mile and 100K finishers.

name: Shark Week Showdown 5K & 10K - Houston
description: An ocean-themed summer race hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Oyster Creek Park in Sugar Land, featuring a kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, and 10K with chip timing and custom finisher medals. Top finishers in the 5K and 10K receive a Bigfoot Trophy, and all participants can expect music and themed festivities.

name: 2nd Annual Run & Brews Fun Run at Katy Beer Garden
description: A fun summer run-and-beer event at Katy Beer Garden offering 1-mile, 5K, and 10K distances with post-race brews in Katy. Venue address to be confirmed on the race website closer to the event.

name: Summer Sizzler - Greater Houston
description: A late-summer Bodies Race Company event at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land offering 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and 15K distances with chip timing, custom finisher medals, designer shirts, Kona Ice, and a kids' dash. Registration by the prior Sunday is required to guarantee a shirt size.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (35)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series, this race at Dow Park in Deer Park offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour limit and themed finisher medals.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (35)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series, this race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: APWA - PWX 2026 - 5K
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Galveston Sand Crab 5K/10K/Kid's Beach 1K
description: A nighttime beach race at Galveston's East Beach Park lit by LED lights. Runners carry flashlights or headlamps on the sand course. Post-race refreshments include beer from Galveston Island Brewing.

name: Beneeezy Purple Monkey Fun Run
description: Annual fundraiser at Alvin High School benefiting college scholarships for graduating Alvin ISD seniors. Features a 1K kids run, 5K, and 10K with live music, entertainment, and finisher medals.

name: Chance for Hope Going Gold Fun Run
description: A timed 5K and 10K supporting the Chance for Hope Foundation, which provides services for children with cancer and their families. Held at Pearland Town Center with music, raffle prizes, and activities.

name: Texas Gatorfest Twilight 5K
description: A twilight 5K run/walk at Fort Anahuac Park as the sun sets, held during the annual Texas Gatorfest weekend. Check-in at 5:00 PM, race at 6:00 PM. Registration $30, closes August 14.

name: Tunnel to Towers 5K Run & Walk - Cypress, TX / Northwest Houston
description: The 4th annual Tunnel to Towers 5K at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, honoring the memory of FDNY firefighter Stephen Siller and saluting local first responders and military. Stroller and wheelchair friendly.

name: 9/11 Heroes Run - Houston, Texas
description: A 5K run and ruck event paying tribute to the heroes of September 11th and honoring those who have served since. Benefits Assist the Officer, IAFF Local 341, and Operation Lone Star.

name: Keller Williams Premier Back to School 5K
description: Annual Labor Day morning 5K hosted by the Katy Area Running Club on a certified, chip-timed course through the Villagio Town Center in Cinco Ranch. Proceeds benefit Ainsley's Angels and The Ballard House. Includes a kids 1K.

name: Run Houston! at University of Houston 5K & 10K presented by Houston Methodist
description: Part of the Run Houston Race Series, this 5K, 10K, and Kids K is set on the University of Houston campus. The series partners with community nonprofits to provide sneakers and socks to underserved children.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (37)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series, this race at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy offers 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (37)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course along a park trail. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals.

name: Night Moves Trail Races
description: Nighttime trail race at Stephen F. Austin State Park hosted by Trail Racing Over Texas. Wide gravel and dirt trails with some elevation changes, suitable for beginners to advanced runners. All participants must carry a light and hydration device.

name: Run for a New Day
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: St. Jude Walk/Run Houston
description: Family-friendly 5K walk/run at Constellation Field during Childhood Cancer Awareness Month. All funds raised support St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, where families never receive a bill. Presented locally by KBR.

name: Toughest 10K Kemah
description: The kickoff race of the Texas Bridge Series, taking runners over the Kemah-Seabrook Bridge with a 3.5% grade and views of Galveston Bay and Clear Lake. Finishes at the Kemah Boardwalk. Strict 2-hour time limit. No strollers or dogs on course.

name: Heroes and Villains Half Marathon - Houston
description: A superhero-and-villain themed race hosted by Bigfoot Running Company in the Sugar Land area, offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, quarter marathon, and half marathon with chip timing, costume contest, and Bigfoot Trophy for top finishers. Venue to be confirmed closer to race day.

name: SUGAR LAND SPACE COWBOYS 5K
description: A running race in Sugar Land, TX.

name: 16th Annual Woodforest Charity Run
description: A 5K, 10K, and kids' dash at Heritage Place Park in downtown Conroe benefiting the Woodforest Charitable Foundation. All net proceeds support the foundation's community programs.

name: University Run at Rice University
description: Formerly the Bayou Bucket, this annual race hosted by Rice University Athletics takes runners through the scenic Rice campus with a jumbotron finish inside Rice Stadium. Includes a 10K, 5K, and Kids K with a post-race pancake breakfast.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (39)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Golden Harvest Half Marathon & 5K/10K - Houston
description: A fall-season road race in Houston offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and half marathon with chip timing, custom finisher medals, and free photos. Produced by Bodies Race Company; venue to be confirmed closer to race day.

name: Galveston Island Shrimp Festival 5K
description: Part of the annual Galveston Island Shrimp Festival, this 5K fun run courses along the Galveston Seawall. Participants receive a performance shirt, finisher medal, and three complimentary beers. A portion of proceeds benefits The Justin Hughes Fund at UTMB.

name: Houston - HFTC 5k
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Zeal RumRunners Race Series
description: A high-energy community timed race series at Meridiana Pkwy in Rosharon, featuring 10K (8:00 AM), 5K (8:15 AM), and a Kids 1K, presented by Zeal Premium Rum. All 21+ finishers receive a complimentary rum cocktail at the post-race celebration.

name: The Rad Race & Rad Fest (Fall Edition)
description: A 5K, 10K, and Kids Dash on the Kingwood Country Club trails, plus a Rad Fest with BMX competition, glow party, bounce houses, vendors, and food. Produced by Rad Racing Company.

name: Ainsley's Angels 9th Annual Twilight 5K
description: A running race in Tomball, TX.

name: Ainsley's Angels 9th Annual Texas Twilight 5K
description: Inclusive evening 5K and family fun run at Tomball Event Center hosted by Ainsley's Angels of America. Features angel pushers who push athletes with disabilities in racing chairs, promoting inclusion for runners and riders of all abilities.

name: Komen Houston Race for the Cure
description: Komen's signature fundraising 5K and 1-mile walk/run at Sam Houston Park along Allen Parkway. Raises money for breast cancer research while celebrating survivors and honoring those affected by the disease.

name: Vallensons Brewing 5K Beer Run
description: Part of the Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge, this chip-timed out-and-back 5K starts and finishes at Vallensons Brewing Co. Finishers receive a custom glass and craft beer tastings. Benefits the Forever Parks Foundation of Pearland.

name: Haunted 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (41)
description: Part of the US Road Running Haunted series at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with themed finisher medals. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Day of the Dead Half Marathon - Galveston
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Sugar Land Half Marathon, 10K & Mile
description: Race #2 of the 2026 Lone Star Race Series, featuring a flat, fast single-loop course starting and finishing at Smart Financial Centre. Sponsored by Houston Methodist Orthopedics & Sports Medicine.

name: Space City 10 Miler and 5K
description: Annual HARRA Fall Series race featuring a scenic certified loop through the Clear Lake area, starting and finishing at the University of Houston-Clear Lake campus. Cash prizes for top finishers in the 10 Mile.

name: 40th Annual Houston Heights Fun Run
description: The 40th annual Houston Heights Fun Run starting and finishing at Marmion Park on Heights Boulevard. Features a 10K, 5K, 5K Walk, and Kids Fun Run (~1K). A beloved Heights neighborhood tradition since 1984.

name: Breast Reconstruction Awareness Close the Loop 5K 2026
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Child Advocates Superhero Run
description: Annual superhero-themed run at CityCentre benefiting Child Advocates, which supports over 2,000 children in foster care in Harris County. Since 2013, the event has raised over $850,000.

name: Shipley Donut Dash
description: Donut-themed fun run at Sam Houston Race Park with on-course donut stations and a Shipley donut at the finish line. A portion of registration benefits Kids' Meals Houston.

name: Day of the Dead Half Marathon - Houston
description: A Día de los Muertos–themed half marathon hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land, offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, quarter marathon, and half marathon. Features a pre-race costume contest, DJ, Bigfoot trophies for top finishers, and a festive post-race celebration at the Race Rally Tent.

name: Haunted 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (42)
description: Part of the US Road Running Haunted series at Dow Park in Deer Park, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with themed finisher medals. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Miles 4 Matthew 5K/10K
description: Charity race at No Label Brewing in Katy honoring Matthew, a young boy who fought leukemia. Proceeds provide financial assistance to families facing pediatric cancer.

name: Friends of Mae S. Bruce Library Fun Run
description: A running race in Santa Fe, TX.

name: Galveston Ultra Trail
description: Trail Racing Over Texas brings a unique shoreline trail running experience to Galveston Island. The point-to-point ultra course runs along the Gulf beach with distances from a Kids Run through a 50K.

name: Angels' Race For Space 5K
description: A 5K at Space Center Houston near the Johnson Space Center campus.

name: Toughest 10K Galveston
description: Part of the Texas Bridge Series, this 10K takes runners across the iconic Galveston Causeway with its demanding 72-foot climb at a 3.5% grade. The out-and-back course starts at the Galveston County Daily News offices and loops around Tiki Island.

name: CHORD ENERGY HOUSTON CORPORATE 5K
address: Start Line Location: Eastern Glades (East Memorial Park Loop) - Flat and Fast, One Lap Around Seymour Lieberman Trail Loop
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Haunted 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (43)
description: Part of the US Road Running Haunted series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course with themed finisher medals. No on-site registration; chip-timed with a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Spooky Sprint Houston
description: A Halloween-themed race organized by Bodies Race Company with distances from a Kids Dash up to a half marathon. Features chip-timing, live results, designer shirts, custom finisher medals, free photos, and treats at the finish line.

name: Boo Dash 5K and Kids Fun Run
description: A family-friendly Halloween-themed evening fun run at Clear Lake Recreation Center near Bay Area Blvd. Features a costume contest, finisher medals, trick-or-treating, and a haunted tunnel. The Kids K starts at 5:00 PM followed by the 5K at 5:15 PM. Proceeds benefit the INEOS ICAN Foundation.

name: UTHealth Houston Half Marathon & 10K
description: Hosted by the Houston Striders since 2006, this USATF-certified race starts at Sam Houston Park and follows a mostly flat downtown route along Allen Parkway and through the city. Title-sponsored by UTHealth Houston, the event raises over $1 million for area nonprofits and includes free Saint Arnold beer, IHOP pancakes, photo downloads, and wheelchair/handcycle divisions.

name: West U Halloween Dash and Kids' Fun Run
description: A Halloween-themed 5K and Kids Fun Run in West University Place, a tradition in one of Houston's most walkable inner-loop neighborhoods.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Deer Park, TX (44)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at Dow Park in Deer Park, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Monster Mash
description: Race 7 of 9 in Houston Running Co.'s Houston Holiday Series, held Halloween morning at Karbach Brewing Co. and featuring a 5K, 10K, and kids & family 1K in full costume. One of Houston's most beloved Halloween events with costume judging, post-race live music, beer at Karbach Brewing, and the complete Monster Mash after-party.

name: Medal Madness 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (44)
description: Part of the US Road Running Medal Madness series at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed Halloween-season finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Daily News Press Run - Galveston
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: 2026 Xracers Day Of The Dead 10K (Ride and Run)
description: A Día de los Muertos–themed hybrid event at the beautiful Baytown Nature Center, combining a 5K trail run and a 10K bike-and-run distance. Participants enjoy the park's wildlife, trails, and waterfront scenery; all finishers receive a shirt and medal at the finish line.

name: Race For Permanent Sanctuary
description: A 5K in Sugar Land supporting permanent animal sanctuary efforts.

name: Harvest Marathon Relay
description: A marathon-distance relay race at Rice University, a popular Houston marathon training benchmark typically run in 4-person teams on the Rice outer loop.

name: 'WHATARUN 5K/10K' (Houston, TX)
description: Celebrating 75 years of Whataburger, this 2nd annual race brings burger lovers together for a flat and fast USATF-certified 5K and 10K on Park Row Drive at the Addicks Park & Ride in the Energy Corridor. Limited to 2,000 participants; entry includes officially licensed Whataburger race swag, post-race food truck sampling, and a visit from Whataguy.

name: Explore The Falls Race
description: A blend of road and trail racing around Lake Holcomb in the Falls at Imperial Oaks community. The 5K is one mile road, one mile gravel/grass/trail, and one mile road; the 10K adds four miles of trail. A Chick-fil-A-sponsored 1K Kids Run and a 2-mile walk/run option are also offered.

name: Back Pew Brewing Co 5K Beer Run
description: Part of the Texas 5K 6-Pack Beer Challenge series. Flat and fast out-and-back course through a shady neighborhood starting and finishing at Back Pew Brewing. Entry includes a custom finisher glass, four beer tastings, and a performance T-shirt.

name: Big Tex Backyard Ultra
description: Last-runner-standing backyard ultra at Stephen F. Austin State Park. Runners tackle a 4.17-mile loop every hour on the hour until one remains. Primarily crushed gravel trails with minimal elevation change. Runners completing 24 loops earn a 100-mile buckle.

name: Fall Family Fun Run
description: A running race in Katy, TX.

name: Warriors of Hope - Irene's 5K
description: A running race in Spring, TX.

name: WhataRun 5K/10K: Sponsored by Whataburger (Day 1)
description: Two-day race event at the Addicks Park and Ride in Houston's Energy Corridor, featuring a flat and fast USATF-certified out-and-back course on Park Row Drive. Limited to 2,000 participants per day with a 1K Kids Dash, post-race Whataburger sampling, and officially licensed Whataburger finisher swag.

name: 4th Annual Mighty Oaks Veterans Day Run
description: Annual Veterans Day 5K and 10K at Heritage Park in Conroe benefiting the Mighty Oaks Foundation. Raises awareness about veteran suicide and supports recovery and resiliency programs for military and first-responder communities.

name: Michael J. Fox Foundation Houston Run/Walk
description: Annual 5K run/walk at Rob Fleming Park in The Woodlands benefiting the Michael J. Fox Foundation for Parkinson's Research. All proceeds support research programs to speed a cure for Parkinson's disease.

name: Get Your Rear in Gear - Houston
description: Colon cancer awareness 5K run/walk at Constellation Field in Sugar Land, home of the Sugar Land Space Cowboys. Organized by the Colon Cancer Coalition to raise funds for colon cancer screening and education.

name: WhataRun 5K/10K: Sponsored by Whataburger (Day 2)
description: Day two of the WhataRun weekend at the Addicks Park and Ride in Houston's Energy Corridor. Same flat, fast USATF-certified course on Park Row Drive, limited to 2,000 runners with Whataburger-themed finisher medals and post-race food truck.

name: Turkey Shuffle - Houston
description: A Thanksgiving-themed run hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Oyster Creek Park in Sugar Land, featuring a kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, and 10K with chip timing, custom finisher medals, and DJ Sasquatch. Participants are encouraged to wear their best Turkey Day costumes for the pre-race costume contest.

name: Gobbler 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Katy, TX (46)
description: Part of the US Road Running Gobbler series at Mason Creek Hike & Bike Trail in Katy, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with Thanksgiving-themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Gobbler 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (46)
description: Part of the US Road Running Gobbler series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: La Porte By the Bay Half Marathon
description: The third and final race of the Texas Bridge Series, this half marathon starts at Sylvan Beach Park in La Porte and crosses the iconic Fred Hartman Bridge — whose 440-foot yellow cable towers have been called an open-air cathedral — twice before finishing at Five Points Town Plaza. Organized by 3 Bros Running Company with a strict 4-hour time limit; all finishers receive a medal and post-race celebration with food and cold beer.

name: The Great Houston Pie Run
description: Thanksgiving-themed fun run featuring a beer and mini-pie for every participant. Part of the Houston Holiday Series organized by Houston Running Co. Includes Half Marathon, 10K, 5K, and Kids/Family 1K.

name: Houston 25K (HMSA Classical)
description: 41st annual HARRA-organized race and third event in the Houston Marathon Warm-up Series. Offers 25K, 10 Mile, and 5K distances starting from Wortham Center Fish Plaza with an after-party at Sam Houston Park.

name: HMSA Classical 25K, 10 Mile & 5K
description: The 40th annual Houston 25K (15.5 miles), 10 Mile, and 5K at Sam Houston Park in downtown Houston. The third race in the Houston Marathon Warm-up Series, a key training benchmark for January marathon runners. Hosted by Houston Masters Sports Association.

name: Houston Turkey Dash
description: Thanksgiving morning tradition at Delmar Stadium in Spring Branch organized by Houston Running Co. Offers the widest distance range of any Houston Turkey Trot including a half marathon.

name: The Turkey Trot Presented by Saint Arnold Brewing Co.
description: Downtown Houston's Thanksgiving morning tradition at Saint Arnold Brewing Company, featuring a chip-timed 5K and 10K course with views of the Downtown skyline plus an inclusive 1-mile walk. Multiple wave starts run from 7:00–9:30 AM; proceeds benefit CoolxDad, a Houston nonprofit uplifting fathers and families.

name: Houston Methodist Turkey Trot
description: Houston's longest-running Thanksgiving Day tradition with over 30 years of history. USATF-certified course through the Galleria and Uptown areas featuring 10K, 5K, and Kids Dash events.

name: Katy YMCA Turkey Dash
description: 24th annual YMCA Turkey Dash on Thanksgiving morning featuring 10K, 5K, 1-mile kids race, and 1-mile family walk/run. Finishers receive medals with a post-race party and kids zone.

name: Bay Area Turkey Trot
description: Annual Thanksgiving morning race in the Clear Lake area starting from On the Run Clear Lake. Features Kids 1K at 7:15 AM, 10K at 7:30 AM, and 5K at 7:40 AM.

name: Sugar Land Turkey Trot
description: 40th annual Turkey Trot through the Sugar Creek neighborhood. Features a 5-mile timed race at 8:00 AM, 2.8-mile family walk/jog at 8:30 AM, and 1-mile kids run at 7:30 AM. All finishers receive a commemorative 40th anniversary medal.

name: YMCA Run Thru the Woods
description: 36th annual Thanksgiving morning tradition in The Woodlands starting at the Cynthia Woods Mitchell Pavilion. Features 5-mile, 3-mile, and kids 1-mile races. Has raised over $2.75 million for local youth charities.

name: L3 Turkey Day 5K & Kids Wishbone 1K
description: 10th annual Thanksgiving morning fun run at Kingwood Town Center Park benefiting the L3 Foundation, a nonprofit supporting pediatric cancer patients and their families founded in honor of Landon Ahrendt.

name: Galveston Island Turkey Trot
description: 5th annual Thanksgiving morning 5K along the Galveston Island Seawall with Gulf Coast views. Out-and-back course ending at Menard Park with a post-race party. Benefits HART, promoting health and fitness on the island.

name: Lake Jackson Turkey Trot
description: 13th annual Thanksgiving morning race starting at Lake Jackson Intermediate School. Benefits the Lake Jackson Intermediate and Rasco Middle School band programs.

name: Pecan Grove Turkey Trot
description: Annual Thanksgiving morning 5K and kids 1-mile race at Pecan Grove Plantation in Richmond. Benefits adoptive families in Fort Bend County and the greater Houston area through The Thankful Ones nonprofit.

name: Turkey Trails - Houston
description: Thanksgiving weekend race at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land organized by Bodies Race Company. Chip-timed with live results, custom finisher medals, and free race photos. Offers Kids Dash, 1 Mile, 5K, 10K, and Half Marathon.

name: Turkey Trails - Houston (Sugar Land)
description: A Thanksgiving-weekend celebration hosted by Bodies Race Company at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land, offering kids' dash, 1-mile, 5K, 10K, and half marathon with chip timing and custom finisher medals. The Saturday-after-Thanksgiving timing is perfect for runners who prefer celebrating a bit later in the holiday weekend.

name: Gobbler 5K, 10K & 13.1M at Cypress, TX (48)
description: Part of the US Road Running Gobbler series at John Paul Landing Park in Cypress, offering 5K, 10K, and half marathon on a flat out-and-back course the Saturday after Thanksgiving. No on-site registration; chip-timed with themed finisher medals and a 3.5-hour course limit.

name: Texas City Half Marathon, 10K, 5K & Family Mile
description: The December finale of the Lone Star Race Series, run on the traffic-free Texas City Dike with panoramic Galveston Bay views. The half marathon course traces the full 5-mile dike as an out-and-back, then finishes with a 3.1-mile loop through Texas City's shoreline park — flat and fast throughout.

name: Run Houston! Sugar Land Santa 5K & 10K presented by Houston Methodist
description: The holiday finale of the Run Houston! Race Series by Houston Methodist, starting and finishing at Constellation Field (Sugar Land Space Cowboys stadium). The festive 5K, 10K, and Kids 1K loop paved roads in the stadium district; proceeds benefit Fort Bend Habitat for Humanity.

name: Brazos Bend 100
description: Texas's largest trail race, held the first Saturday of December at Brazos Bend State Park in Needville. Events span 100 miles (a Western States 100 lottery qualifier for sub-32-hour finishers), 100K, marathon, and half marathon on a flat, wildlife-rich multi-loop course through bayou flats shared with American alligators. A free kids race runs at noon Saturday; the finish line closes Sunday at 2 PM.

name: A Charlie Brown Christmas 5K/10K
description: The 5th Annual officially licensed Peanuts event at Addicks Park and Ride in Houston's Energy Corridor. The flat, USATF-certified out-and-back course runs on Park Row Drive. Schedule: Snoopy Kids Dash 8:00 AM, 10K 8:15 AM, 5K 8:20 AM. Capped at 2,000 participants and sold out all four prior years. All finishers receive a limited-edition collector's snow globe medal, performance tee, and numbered Peanuts bib.

name: Last Man Standing
description: A running race in Willis, TX.

name: Run for the House - Ronald McDonald House Charities Greater Houston 5K and Kids 1 Miler
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Gingerbread Lane 5K & 10K Houston
description: A festive holiday trail race at John Paul Landing Park along the Cypress greenbelt, featuring a 5K, 10K, untimed 1 Mile, and Kids Dash (50-meter sprint). DJ Sasquatch provides holiday music on course; post-race includes gingerbread cookies and milk. Costume contest with prizes. Part of the multi-city Bigfoot Running Company Gingerbread Lane series.

name: Trafigura Run for the House
description: The 16th annual USATF-certified 5K and Kids 1-Miler in downtown Houston benefiting Ronald McDonald House Charities Greater Houston. The Trafigura Foundation matches proceeds dollar for dollar up to $300,000.

name: Flapjack Fun Run 5K
description: A 5K fun run in Friendswood, a south-Houston suburb between Pearland and League City.

name: Tavola Reindeer Run
description: A holiday-themed reindeer run in the Tavola community in New Caney, northeast of Houston.

name: Jingle Bell Run Houston
description: A jingle-bell-themed holiday 5K at T.C. Jester Park in northwest Houston.

name: Conroe 10 Miler
description: A 10-mile road race in Conroe, north of Houston. Part of the Texas Bridge Series.

name: Kingwood Christmas Cookie Dash
description: The 3rd Annual Kingwood Christmas Cookie Dash on the trails of Kingwood Country Club, co-located with the Kingwood Christmas Market. Offers 5K, 10K, a unique 10K relay (4 legs of 1.5 miles each — runners decorate a cookie before tagging off), and Kids Dash. Benefiting Lily's Toy Box, Toys for Tots, and Angel Tree Houston. NOTE: The 2026 edition is already sold out.

name: Candy Cane Course - Houston
description: A running race in Sugar Land, TX.

name: Rudolph Fun Run
description: A Rudolph-themed holiday fun run at CityCentre in west Houston.

name: Reindeer Fun Run
description: A reindeer-themed holiday 5K in Nassau Bay, near the Clear Lake/NASA area.

name: Coach Andy Stewart's 30K & 10K
description: A 30K (18.6 miles) and 10K in Sugar Land, a key final long-run training race for Houston Marathon runners in January. Named for legendary Houston running coach Andy Stewart.

name: Houston's 12K of Christmas
description: Houston Running Co.'s 14th-annual holiday tradition at Sam Houston Park in downtown, offering a 12K and companion 6K through the city's historic core. The festive post-race party includes hot cocoa, Santa Claus appearances, Christmas carolers, and Karbach beer; also serves as the final race in the Houston Holiday Series.

name: Santa Hustle Galveston Half Marathon, 10K, 5K & Kids Dash
description: Holiday-themed race along the Galveston Seawall featuring half marathon, 10K, 5K, and kids dash distances. Participants run in Santa gear with holiday music, Christmas decorations, and cookies and hot cocoa at the finish line.

name: Candy Cane Course
description: A candy-cane-themed holiday 5K in Sugar Land.

name: Houston Resolution Run
description: A year-end race at Fish Plaza in downtown Houston with flexible start times. Offers a Nearly Half Marathon, 10K, 5K, and Family 1 Mile. A popular way to close out the running year and set New Year's resolutions.

name: Texas Marathon Kingwood
description: A New Year's Day tradition since 2000, run entirely on the concrete greenbelt trails of Kingwood winding past Kingwood Lake. The marathon is four loops and the half marathon two loops on a USATF-certified course; capped at 700 runners and operated by the Knights of Columbus with proceeds supporting children's charities.

name: 48th Annual Lone Star Stampede
description: The 48th annual Texas Exes Houston Chapter scholarship run at Brazos River Park, featuring a 5K, 10K, kids dash, and — new for 2027 — a first-ever half marathon. A Houston tradition for UT Austin alumni and supporters, with proceeds funding college scholarships for Houston-area students.

name: Bigfoot Classic Houston
description: Annual multi-distance race hosted by Bigfoot Running Company at Brazos River Park in Sugar Land. Features a flat course through scenic parkland along the Brazos River with distances from a Kids Dash to 15K.

name: ChocoLoco 5K and 10K
description: Houston's original chocolate-themed race at Sam Houston Park in downtown. Features chip-timed racing, finisher medals, and a post-race celebration with hot cocoa and chocolate fountains.

name: Run Houston! Sam Houston Race Park 5K & 10K
description: Part of the Run Houston Race Series presented by Houston Methodist. A family-friendly event at Sam Houston Race Park featuring a flat, fast course with 5K, 10K, and Kids 1K options.

name: We Are Houston 5K
description: The We Are Houston 5K presented by Aramco and Chevron is the Saturday kickoff to Houston Marathon weekend, starting at McKinney and Crawford near the George R. Brown Convention Center. Runners who complete this 5K plus the Sunday marathon or half earn the Houston Double and receive three finisher medals.

name: Chevron Houston Marathon & Aramco Houston Half Marathon
description: The 55th Annual Chevron Houston Marathon and 26th Annual Aramco Houston Half Marathon run on a flat, fast downtown course starting at Congress and Fannin near Minute Maid Park. The USATF-certified marathon is a Boston Marathon qualifier, with a combined event cap of approximately 27,000 participants finishing at Lamar and Crawford by the George R. Brown Convention Center.

name: SKECHERS Hot Chocolate Run Houston
description: America's sweetest road race starting and finishing at Sam Houston Park, with the course running along Allen Parkway beside Buffalo Bayou. Offered in 5K (7:15 AM), 10K, and 10-mile distances; every finisher receives a chocolate fondue mug with dippables, a commemorative full-zip SKECHERS jacket, and a finisher medal.

name: Run the Distance for Kylie - 5th Annual Event
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Surfside Beach Marathon
description: Annual beach marathon run entirely on the sand between Surfside Beach and San Luis Pass, one of the only fully-on-sand marathons in the world. Start and finish at Stahlman Park Pavilion, with marathon and half marathon at 7:30 a.m., Kids K at 8:00 a.m., and 10K/5K at 8:15 a.m. An early-bird walker start is offered at 5:30 a.m.

name: Rocky Raccoon 100
description: Long-running Tejas Trails ultramarathon at Huntsville State Park that sends runners on 5 x 20-mile loops of flat, pine-forest singletrack around Lake Raven. 100-mile finishers under 30 hours earn a spot in the Western States lottery. Includes a 100K, 100 Mile, and a kids' 1-mile trail run. Base at Raven Lodge.

name: 2027 Xracers Trail Run Year Of The Goat
description: A running race in Baytown, TX.

name: Houston Ultra Trail
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: LoveStruck 5K/10K
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Rodeo Clown Roundup
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: The Woodlands 5K & 2K Fun Run
description: Companion 5K and family 2K Fun Run held one week before The Woodlands Marathon. Both events start and finish on the south side of the Cynthia Woods Mitchell Pavilion along the Woodlands Waterway.

name: Galveston Marathon & Half Marathon
description: A running race in Galveston, TX.

name: Tomball ISD Superintendent's Fun Run
description: A running race in Tomball, TX.

name: The Woodlands Marathon
description: The Woodlands Marathon is a Boston-qualifying road race starting and finishing at Town Green Park, with a flat course through The Woodlands' tree-lined streets and village paths. The race weekend offers a full marathon, half marathon, 10K, and 2- or 4-person marathon relay. Billed as one of the fastest marathon courses in Texas.

name: KOLACHE FACTORY 5K/10K/KIDS DASH
address: Start Line Location: Addicks Park and Ride (Energy Corridor, Right Off I-10) - Flat and Fast Course on Park Row Drive
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Seabrook Lucky Trails
description: A running race in Seabrook, TX.

name: Midas FA 5K Race Against Childhood Obesity
description: A running race in TBA, TX.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
address: Terry Hershey Park (west of Eldridge Pkwy)
description: Free, weekly, timed 5K along the hike-and-bike trail in Terry Hershey Park in west Houston's Energy Corridor. Open to all ages and paces; register once with parkrun for a barcode to get results.

name: Texas Independence Relay
address: Saint Arnold Brewing Company, 2000 Lyons Ave
description: Team relay of roughly 200 miles split across 36 legs, starting Saturday morning in Gonzales and finishing Sunday at Saint Arnold Brewing Company in Houston. Traces the route of Sam Houston and the Texian Army from the 1835 "Come and Take It" skirmish to Houston.

name: The Ascent 5k Race
description: A running race in The Woodlands, TX.

name: ‘RUNNING WITH MY PEEPS 5K/10K’ (Houston, TX)
address: Start Line Location: Addicks Park and Ride (Energy Corridor, Right Off I-10) - Flat and Fast Course on Park Row Drive
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Terry Hershey parkrun
description: A free, weekly timed 5K community run held every Saturday at 8:00 AM in Terry Hershey Park along the Buffalo Bayou trail in west Houston. Open to runners of all abilities; one-time parkrun registration required, but no entry fee.

name: Spindle Tree parkrun
description: A free, weekly timed 5K held every Saturday at 7:30 AM on the Spindle Tree Jogging Trail in Magnolia, northwest of The Woodlands. Course consists of three loops on a purpose-built neighborhood trail; one-time parkrun registration required.

name: Bellaire Trolley Run
description: Annual community 5K and 1-mile race hosted by the City of Bellaire, Patrons for Bellaire Parks, and the Al Lawrence Running Club. The course starts and finishes at Bellaire Town Square and includes wheelchair and stroller divisions.

name: Art Car IPA 5K presented by UHD
description: A running race in Houston, TX.

name: Annual 5K Butterfly Run!
description: A running race in Katy, TX.

name: Race for the Dome
description: The 6th annual 5K road race around the Houston Astrodome and through NRG Park, benefiting the Astrodome Conservancy. A 1K kids fun run precedes the 5K, followed by a celebration marking the Astrodome's 61st birthday.
